target.title: typingMode 'instant' never fires for queued/steered turns; Telegram room_event unconditionally suppresses typing
target.body:
## Summary

Two related gaps make `agents.defaults.typingMode: "instant"` silently ineffective for the message-tool delivery setups the docs themselves recommend. Verified on 2026.6.5 (macOS) and confirmed unchanged on current main.

## Gap 1 â€” queued/steered follow-up turns never start typing (primary)

`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner` has two run paths:
- `runReplyAgent` (fresh turns): calls `typingSignals.signalRunStart()` at run start â†’ typing works.
- `createFollowupRunner` (turns queued/steered under `messages.queue.mode: "steer"`): creates the same typing signaler but **never calls `signalRunStart`**. Its only typing trigger is streamed renderable text â€” which `message_tool`-delivery turns never produce (their streamed final is the silent token; visible output goes through `message(action="send")`).

Result: with long-running sessions, most real inbound messages arrive while a run is active, take the follow-up path, and show no typing at all. Log audit on an affected install: 42 Telegram `sendMessage` operations in a day, **0 `sendChatAction`** â€” DMs and groups alike. A direct Bot API `sendChatAction` with the same token renders fine, so it's purely that the loop is never started.

Docs (`concepts/typing-indicators.md`) define `instant` as "start typing **as soon as the model loop begins**, even if the run later returns only the silent reply token" â€” with no queued-turn exception.

Suggested fix: call `typingSignals.signalRunStart()` at follow-up run start in `createFollowupRunner`, mirroring `runReplyAgent` (cleanup via the existing `markRunComplete`/`markDispatchIdle` already works).

## Gap 2 â€” Telegram hardcodes `suppressTyping` for room events

`extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` sets `suppressTyping: isRoomEvent` unconditionally, while pairing it with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` â€” the exact mode `resolveTypingMode()` deliberately maps to `"instant"`. The suppression check runs first, so that mapping (and any explicitly configured `typingMode`) is unreachable for unmentioned group turns.

This contradicts the docs three ways:
1. `channels/ambient-room-events.md` calls `unmentionedInbound: "room_event"` + `visibleReplies: "message_tool"` "the recommended mode" for always-on group chats.
2. `concepts/typing-indicators.md` documents `typingMode` with no room-event exception.
3. The ambient-room-events troubleshooting section itself describes debugging "typing â€¦ with no visible room message" â€” i.e. the docs expect typing to occur during room events.

A quiet default for ambient rooms is reasonable; the issue is that it also overrides an explicit user-configured `typingMode`. Suggested fix: `suppressTyping: isRoomEvent && <no explicit typingMode configured>`, or a `messages.groupChat.roomEventTyping` knob. (Discord's handler appears to share the same pattern.)

## Repro

1. `agents.defaults.typingMode: "instant"`, `messages.queue.mode: "steer"`, Telegram channel, `messages.groupChat: { unmentionedInbound: "room_event", visibleReplies: "message_tool" }`.
2. Send a DM while a session run is active â†’ no `sendChatAction` ever issued (gap 1).
3. Send an unmentioned group message while idle â†’ no `sendChatAction` (gap 2).

Both verified by absence of any `sendChatAction`/typing-loop entries in gateway logs while `sendMessage` operations log normally.

We've applied both one-line fixes locally and they restore documented behavior without affecting message-tool final delivery.
target.author: binhdoan123
